transport: one WebSocket per browser; all tabs/iframes multiplexed over it, addressed by `context`; commands correlated by `id` (the channel physics).

session.status.does: is the session alive + the protocol responsive (the channel's liveness)

session.subscribe.does: subscribe to events — WITHOUT this the channel emits nothing (one socket, but silent until subscribed)

browsingContext.create.does: open YOUR OWN new tab (an isolated surface) — returns its context id; pass it in later calls so you do not touch other clients' tabs

browsingContext.navigate.does: go to a url in a context

browsingContext.captureScreenshot.does: screenshot a context (viewport keeps it small)

script.evaluate.does: run JS in a context — type, click, read the DOM, post to a peer

input.performActions.does: pointer/key action sequences — tap, swipe, drag, type (a real-world gesture, not the wire's)

events._note: events are first-class — the channel describes what you RECEIVE (the afferent half), not only what you send. Subscribe via session.subscribe, read the stream via GET <broker>/events.

network.beforeRequestSent.does: a request is about to leave (efferent)

verify.live: session.status (or browsingContext.getTree) succeeds -> the socket is alive and the protocol responds
verify.method_exists: send the method; a BiDi/JSON-RPC error code -32601 (unknown command) is the channel's 404 (absent); a params/validation error means the method EXISTS but the args were wrong. The probe verdict beats this prior, always.
